prompt: I approved my port turn-up spec. Design the solution. The environment has a ServiceNow adapter running and devices in inventory.

expectations.text: Authenticates and pulls bootstrap data AFTER spec approval (not before)
expectations.evidence: Response explicitly states 'you've approved the spec, so we're past Gate 1' and then proceeds to Phase 2 (Discover) starting with authentication. No API calls were described before Gate 1. Auth and bootstrap pulls happen in Steps 2A and 2B, which follow the approved spec.

expectations.text: Pulls spec-contingent data based on approved scope (devices, workflows)
expectations.evidence: Step 2C explicitly pulls devices.json (because port turn-up involves device operations and user confirmed devices in inventory) and workflows.json (to find reuse candidates). Both are spec-contingent pulls triggered by the approved scope, not pulled blindly.

expectations.text: Resolves adapter names from apps.json not tasks.json
expectations.evidence: Step 2E explicitly states: 'I resolve the correct adapter app name from apps.json (not from tasks.json, since task names have incorrect casing for adapters).' This matches the skill's gotcha and CLAUDE.md rule #3.

expectations.text: Produces component inventory with type and action (build/reuse/skip)
expectations.evidence: Section D of the design output includes a Component Inventory table with columns for #, Component, Type (Command Template, Child Workflow, Workflow Task, Parent Workflow, Jinja2 Template), and Action (Build or Reuse). Each component has a clear action designation.

expectations.text: Maps acceptance criteria to test methods
expectations.evidence: Section F explicitly maps each acceptance criterion (port comes up with correct VLAN, ServiceNow ticket created, rollback on failure) to a specific test method (run job and verify interface status, verify ticket via adapter call, inject failure and verify rollback).

expectations.text: Presents design for approval (Gate 2) before building
expectations.evidence: Response ends with explicit Gate 2 presentation: 'Here is the full solution design. Review each section. When you approve, I'll begin building...' and states 'I do NOT proceed to Phase 4 (Build) until you explicitly approve this design.'
